chunk_summary: Guidance on food safety counselling for THR and hot cooked meals in MAM, emphasizing hygiene, safe storage, preparation, and feeding practices to prevent diarrhoea and ensure effective nutritional support.
learning_objectives: Understand food safety practices for take-home rations (THR) and hot cooked meals (HCM) in MAM management.
Learn diarrhoea management strategies, including rehydration and zinc supplementation, in malnourished children.
Identify hygiene and storage protocols to prevent contamination and ensure safe feeding.
Audit caregiver practices to improve food preparation and child feeding outcomes.
key_facts: Hot cooked meals should be fed fresh and reheated thoroughly if stored.
Take-home rations must be stored in dry, covered containers and prepared with safe water.
Handwashing with soap before food preparation and feeding is essential to prevent contamination.
Zinc supplementation for acute diarrhoea: 20 mg daily for 10–14 days (or 10 mg daily if under 6 months).
ORS should be used for rehydration in MAM unless severe wasting or oedema is present.
Discard rations with foul smell, visible insects, or moisture clumping to avoid illness.
reference_quote: Supplementary Nutrition Rules under ICDS emphasize safe storage and preparation practices to ensure effective nutritional support — Government of India, 2017.
document_key_topics: Food safety counselling for THR and HCM
Hygiene and storage protocols for supplementary feeding
